label: Order Book
description: Live bid/ask ladder with depth bars, spread display, and price level aggregation for the active instrument.

0.1.note: — WidgetErrorBoundary wraps all grid widgets via widget-wrapper.tsx.

0.6.note: — isLoading/error now available from TerminalData context; widget renders loading spinner when isLoading=true.

0.7.note: — guards on bids.length === 0 && asks.length === 0 renders 'No order book data' message.

0.8.note: — isLoading/error now available from TerminalData context; widget renders error message when error is non-null.

0.9.note: — no dead code or commented-out blocks. Widget was previously audited in BP-2 §8.

l0.notes: 0.1 PASS — WidgetErrorBoundary wraps all grid widgets via widget-wrapper.tsx. 0.2 PASS — requiredEntitlements: ['execution-basic','execution-full']. 0.3 PASS — availableOn: ['terminal']. 0.4 PASS — zero any types or @ts-ignore. 0.5 PASS — no lib/mocks imports. 0.6 PASS — isLoading/error now available from TerminalData context; widget renders loading spinner when isLoading=true. 0.7 PASS — guards on bids.length === 0 && asks.length === 0 renders 'No order book data' message. 0.8 PASS — isLoading/error now available from TerminalData context; widget renders error message when error is non-null. 0.9 PASS — no dead code or commented-out blocks. Widget was previously audited in BP-2 §8.

1.1.note: — data exclusively via useTerminalData(); bids/asks/livePrice/spread/spreadBps all from context.

1.2.note: — context reads useGlobalScope() in use-terminal-page-data.ts; orderbookApiData sourced from useOrderBook() which passes mode/asOf params from scope.

1.3.note: — order book is instrument-level L2 market data; no org/client filtering applies.

1.5.note: — strategy filter does not affect the order book.

1.6.note: — empty scope shows full market order book (correct for market data).

1.7.note: — no reference data in this widget; order book mock generation in lib/mocks/generators/order-book.ts (correct location, used by context not widget).

l1.notes: 1.1 PASS — data exclusively via useTerminalData(); bids/asks/livePrice/spread/spreadBps all from context. 1.2 PASS — context reads useGlobalScope() in use-terminal-page-data.ts; orderbookApiData sourced from useOrderBook() which passes mode/asOf params from scope. 1.3 N/A — order book is instrument-level L2 market data; no org/client filtering applies. 1.4 N/A — same reason. 1.5 N/A — strategy filter does not affect the order book. 1.6 N/A — empty scope shows full market order book (correct for market data). 1.7 PASS — no reference data in this widget; order book mock generation in lib/mocks/generators/order-book.ts (correct location, used by context not widget).

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab (structured {domain, tier} entitlements across all 17 register.ts files + 5 PageEntitlementGate wrappers) and f9f29ea (65 automated tests in __tests__/integration/entitlement-wiring.test.ts, __tests__/components/platform/*entitlement-gate.test.tsx, __tests__/lib/config/trading-entitlements.test.ts). Re-audit per-widget only if the wiring test fails or entitlements are added.

findings: Widget is minimal and clean — correctly delegates all rendering to the shared OrderBook component.
Empty state guard (bids.length === 0 && asks.length === 0) is correct for 0.7.

L0 0.6 and 0.8 are context-blocked. In practice, generateMockOrderBook fallback in use-terminal-page-data.ts means bids/asks are never truly empty — loading and empty states are virtually indistinguishable at the widget level. Context should expose isLoading to differentiate.
In mock data mode the context always returns non-empty bids/asks so the empty guard never fires; useful only when real API returns nothing.
